title: Project Freedom: 1,500 Ships Stuck, 2 Cleared, One 'Dome'
narrative: The Pentagon and Trump administration claim 'Project Freedom' has cleared a safe shipping lane through the Strait of Hormuz. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth called the US military presence a 'red, white and blue dome' protecting commerce. Joint Chiefs Chairman Gen. Dan Caine said the lane is clear and attacks 'do not meet the threshold' of violating the ceasefire. The public message: the US has opened the waterway and commerce can resume. The physical evidence: 1,500 ships remain stuck in the Persian Gulf, including approximately 1,000 large ocean-going vessels. Only 2 US-flag ships crossed on the first day. Iran publicly states it still controls the strait and non-approved routes will face 'decisive response.' The Pentagon's own logic — attacks don't meet the ceasefire-violation threshold — is an Orwellian redefinition: ongoing attacks are reframed as non-violations because they do not cross a threshold the US unilaterally set. European allies refuse to participate until a 'permanent ceasefire' — an open-ended condition Iran controls. Brent crude at $109/barrel, up 44% since the war began.
telemetry: Only 2 US-flag ships crossed the Strait of Hormuz on the first day of the so-called 'cleared lane' — the operational reality against the administration's announcement.
~1,500 ships remain stuck in the Persian Gulf, including ~1,000 large ocean-going vessels, waiting for conditions that have not materialized.
Iran publicly states it still controls the strait and non-approved routes will face 'decisive response' — directly contradicting the Pentagon's 'open' narrative.
The Pentagon's framing — attacks don't meet the ceasefire-violation threshold — is a unilateral redefinition of what counts as a violation: ongoing physical attacks are reframed as non-violations.
European allies (UK, France) refuse to participate in the 'Project Freedom' corridor until a 'permanent ceasefire' — an open-ended condition Iran effectively controls.
Brent crude at $109/barrel, up 44% since the war began; WTI at $105 — oil price gains happened the same week Trump's rhetoric 'dented hopes' of a Hormuz deal, not after a commercial corridor opened.
verdict: [SIPHONED VERDICT]: The administration says the lane is open. The evidence says otherwise. 1,500 ships versus 2 ships cleared, Iran's ongoing claim of strait control, European refusal to participate, and oil prices that moved on war rhetoric — not on the announcement of a cleared corridor — tell a consistent physical story: the Strait of Hormuz is not functioning as an open waterway. 'Project Freedom' is a political narrative layered over an operational reality that has not changed.
